# @verevoir/qr

QR code encoding engine and SVG renderers. Zero runtime dependencies. Pure TypeScript.

## Why use this over node-qrcode?

node-qrcode pulls in pngjs, dijkstrajs, and yargs as runtime dependencies. It produces one visual style (basic square grid), returns a single mask variant, and requires the `canvas` npm package for many operations.

@verevoir/qr has zero runtime dependencies, works identically in Node and browsers, and offers:

- 12 visual styles: square, dots, diamonds, horizontal, vertical, diagonal, network, circuit, metro, scribble, photo, logo
- photo and logo styles sample a user-supplied image via a curried PhotoSampler — photo modulates dot density from local luminance, logo sparsely overlays dots on a composited source image (cull thresholds per ISO/IEC 15415). `imageToSampler` in `@verevoir/qr/web` wraps any CanvasImageSource. Neither surface via the node-qrcode shim (its API can't carry a sampler callback).
- Multi-candidate mask selection (all masks within 30% of best penalty score)
- `logoArea` encode option reserves matrix capacity for a centre-covering logo — forces H-level EC and bumps the version until the data occupies at most `1 - logoArea` of the available bytes. Recommended range 0.05–0.25; above ~0.30 exceeds H's recovery budget.
- Fabrication-ready SVG output (closed filled paths for laser cutting, CNC, vinyl)
- Full colour control (dark, light, background)
- Two corner styles for finder patterns (square, rounded)
- Line width control (normal/thin) across all styles
- PNG output via optional @resvg/resvg-js peer dep (lazy-imported, tree-shakes out if unused)

Drop-in compatible: `import QRCode from '@verevoir/qr/qrcode'` replaces `import QRCode from 'qrcode'` with the same API surface (create, toString, toFile, toBuffer, toCanvas, toDataURL).

## Quick start

```ts
import { encode, toSvg } from '@verevoir/qr';

const candidates = encode('https://example.com');
const svg = toSvg(candidates[0], {
  style: 'network',
  cornerStyle: 'rounded',
  lineWidth: 'thin',
  color: { dark: '#1a1a2e', background: '#ffffff' },
});
```

## Styles

| Style | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| square | Filled squares per module (default) |
| dots | Round dots — dark and light layers for image overlay |
| diamonds | Diamond-shaped modules rotated 45 degrees |
| horizontal | Horizontal line segments for consecutive dark modules |
| vertical | Vertical line segments |
| diagonal | Diagonal line segments |
| network | Connected traced paths with diamond tips at endpoints |
| circuit | Connected traced paths with circular tips |
| metro | Layered horizontal, vertical and diagonal lines |
| scribble | Connected component walking with bezier-smoothed turns |
| photo | Dot-density modulated by a luminance sampler; light modules in dark regions get a dark ring + small light centre |
| logo | Sparse dots overlaid on a composited image; ISO/IEC 15415 two-threshold cull (default lum<0.4 / lum>0.7) |

All styles respect the `lineWidth` option ('normal' or 'thin') and produce scannable output.

## API

### Main entry (`@verevoir/qr`)

- `encode(text, options?)` — returns `QrResult[]` (multiple mask candidates)
- `toSvg(qr, options?)` — renders a QrResult as an SVG string

Options: `{ style, cornerStyle, lineWidth, color: { dark, light, background } }`

### Node (`@verevoir/qr/node`)

- `toFile(path, text, options?)` — write SVG or PNG (by extension)
- `toBuffer(text, options?)` — return SVG or PNG Buffer

PNG requires `@resvg/resvg-js` as an optional peer dependency.

### Browser (`@verevoir/qr/web`)

- `svgToPng(svg, size?)` — render SVG to PNG Blob via canvas
- `downloadPng(svg, filename?, size?)` — trigger PNG file download
- `createSvgElement(svg)` — parse SVG string to DOM element
- `renderIntoElement(container, svg)` — insert SVG into a container
- `imageToSampler(source)` — build a `PhotoSampler` from any `CanvasImageSource` for the `photo` and `logo` styles

### node-qrcode shim (`@verevoir/qr/qrcode`)

Drop-in replacement for the `qrcode` npm package. Same API: `create`, `toString`, `toFile`, `toBuffer`, `toCanvas`, `toDataURL`. Supports callback and Promise forms.

## Architecture

- `src/encode.ts` — top-level encoder orchestrating version selection, data encoding, matrix construction, mask evaluation
- `src/svg/trace.ts` — per-cell DFS walker producing vertex paths from cell grids
- `src/svg/outline.ts` — cell-based and trace-based renderers (renderCells, renderOutline, renderCircuit)
- `src/svg/index.ts` — style multiplexer (toSvg dispatches to renderers, adds corners, wraps SVG)
- `src/svg/corners.ts` — finder and alignment pattern rendering

Zero runtime dependencies. ~52KB core bundle (ESM). TypeScript source with full .d.ts output.
